paragraphs: What did Reuters say Lionel Messi did in Argentina's two key attacking moments?
Reuters said Messi provided the assist for Enzo Fernandez's equalizer. It also said he supplied the cross leading to Lautaro Martinez's winning goal.
An assist names the player who sets up a goal. A cross leading to a goal points to an earlier action in the same attack. Neither description changes the names of the players who finished.

This is why the report offers more than a score. It asks listeners to separate the final touch from the actions that made that touch possible. In this story, the question is not who mattered more. It is what each player did.
Argentina defeated England 2-1 in the World Cup semifinal on July 15, 2026. The result advanced Argentina to the final against Spain.

subsections.heading: Messi's Creating Role
paragraphs: According to Reuters, Messi was involved in both of Argentina's key attacking moments. He provided the assist when Enzo Fernandez equalized. He then supplied the cross leading to Lautaro Martinez's winning goal.

Reuters therefore gives Messi a clear creating role. The report does not say that he scored either goal. Instead, it connects him to the pass for the equalizer and to the cross before the winner. That wording helps listeners hear the path of each attack, not only its final result.
For a comeback story, that is an important detail. The player who starts or shapes a move can be central even when another player completes it.

subsections.heading: The Finishing Names
paragraphs: Fernandez and Martinez were the players who scored Argentina's two goals. Fernandez equalized, and Martinez scored the winning goal in the 92nd minute.

Their names answer a different question: who finished the attacks? In a match report, goal scorers and chance creators may be different players. Here, Reuters connected Messi to two earlier actions while identifying Fernandez and Martinez as the scorers.
That separation keeps a listener from treating every part of a scoring move as the same role. The equalizer and the winner each have a creator-side description and a scorer-side description.

sections.heading: In One Line
paragraphs: Reuters credited Messi with creating the equalizer and the winning move, while Fernandez and Martinez scored the goals.
The report is not only about a 2-1 win. It is also about how language divides an attacking move into creation and finishing.
Listen for both parts: the player who makes the chance and the player who completes it.
